events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms
events_with_summaries.summary_text: The Easter Safety Sprint runs April 3 through 6. With holiday traffic picking up, let’s keep the store safe and ready. Do quick safety sweeps, check and restock spill stations and PPE, and review spring weather readiness on every shift. Be sure to share your participation on Workvivo with the hashtag eastersafety.

events_with_summaries.title: Dept. 93: Easter Week is Here!
events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms
events_with_summaries.summary_text:
Easter Week is Here! Let’s Finish Strong

Easter week is here and we need to deliver a strong finish for customers. Focus on Easter favorites: turkeys, hams, Cornish hens, boneless rib roasts and whole tenderloins. Keep bunkers full and stocked throughout the day to drive sales. Hold steady on Easter hams: The Saturday before Easter is one of the biggest sales days of the season with the average store turning the Marketside ham bunker about five times, so stock strategically. Avoid early markdowns on fresh turkeys: Merchants will monitor sell-through and support post-Easter pricing as needed, with whole fresh turkeys dropping to fifty cents per pound after Easter. Finally, protect quality with tight temperature control: Target 30 degrees; at 34 degrees, shelf life starts to drop, losing one day for each degree the temp raises. If browning or leaks are visible, check the temperatures. Review the time-lapse temperature video for examples.

events_with_summaries.title: Dept. 98 & 81: WK10 Bakery & Bread Updates
events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms
events_with_summaries.summary_text:
Dept. 98: Fresh Bakery Easter Sell-Through, Markdowns and Inventory Management

Keep Easter items full through Sunday, April 5, then on Monday, April 6 transition back to your core assortment and set strong everyday features. Adjust production levels down so not to overproduce and be sure to keep your presentation clean during the conversion. Shift features to high-velocity strong core sellers. Consider featuring Freshness Guaranteed 10-count cookies, 8-count cinnamon rolls, and 6-inch cheesecakes and 4-count Marketside muffins.

Dept. 81: Commercial Bread Post-Easter Recovery

Return features and displays back to normal levels on Monday, April 6. Work with your DSD suppliers to adjust bread/roll inventory levels down according to your everyday customer demand. Finally, be sure to keep the Brown & Serve Rolls home location full, and don't over stock.

events_with_summaries.title: AI Tip of the Week
events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms
events_with_summaries.summary_text:
Keep Calm and Ask My Assistant - AI Tip of the Week

Enter a real, specific problem into My Assistant and ask for solutions. AI works better when given a real issue, not just generic questions.

Example: "Our store has long checkout lines between 5-7 p.m. and limited staff. What are five practical ways we can improve checkout flow without adding more associates?"

events_with_summaries.title: New Restroom Disinfectant and Soap Dispenser Test Update
events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms
events_with_summaries.summary_text:
Backroom:

Restroom disinfectant is shifting from Oxivir to TB disinfectant. Orders for Oxivir have stopped, and remaining supply should be used before fully transitioning by April 15 or as inventory runs out.

The Diversey soap dispenser test has ended. Dispensers in select stores should be removed and replaced with Ecolab units. Stores should confirm their status and follow up with Ecolab if replacements are not complete. Use the lookup tool on the Weekly Message page to verify if your store was part of this test.

events_with_summaries.title: Total Fresh: Prep for April Fresh Cost Inventory
events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms
events_with_summaries.summary_text:
Prep for April Fresh Cost Inventory

Fresh Cost Inventory is Tuesday, April 28. There are three tasks for salaried managers to complete. First, ensure proper staffing is in place to conduct Fresh Cost Inventory counts and provide post-inventory customer service. Second, visit the Fresh OneWalmart page. Here, you'll need to access the Ops Portal Inventory Tracking Report (ITR) to identify any potential errors, and develop a plan to resolve them. At the bottom of the page, you'll find your beginning inventory numbers (which are updated the Saturday prior to Cost Inventory). Finally, refer to the Cost Inventory section of the Fresh Better Way guide to learn more about inventory preparation, how to count, digital finalization rules for truck invoices, the finalization process, the inventory schedule and proper execution. If you have any process questions, email eomifs@email.wal-mart.com. For technical issues, submit a ticket in the MyWalmart app under Facility Management and select the Walmart Cost Inventory app.
